user_prompt: I think you're scoring too many of these as Tier 1. Tighten the criteria — only true high-match.
actual_output:
### Tier 1 Criteria — Tightened

**Previous criteria (too loose):**
- Senior frontend role ✓
- React + TypeScript ✓
- $150K+ ✓
- NYC-based ✓

This pulled in 18 jobs because nearly every listing met these baseline requirements.

**Revised Tier 1 criteria (all must be true):**

| Criterion | Threshold |
|-----------|-----------|
| Stack alignment | 4+ of 5 core technologies match (React, TypeScript, Next.js, GraphQL, plus their specific extras) |
| Seniority fit | Role explicitly seeks 5-8 years (not "senior-to-staff" or "principal") |
| Salary signal | Posted range starts at $170K+ OR company known to pay above market |
| Company traction | Series B+ with recent funding, or profitable public co — no early seed, no layoff-heavy orgs |
| Application depth | Standard application (no take-home > 4 hours, no 6-round loops listed) |
| Differentiator | Something specific I can authentically reference (product I've used, engineering blog I've read, open source they maintain) |

### Re-Scored Results

**Tier 1 — High Match: 6 jobs** (down from 18)
- Stripe, Ramp, Linear, Vercel, Figma, Notion

**Tier 2 — Good Match: 31 jobs** (was 24)
- The 12 demoted from Tier 1 moved here

**Tier 3 — Worth a Shot: 17 jobs** (was 12)

### Why These 6 Cleared the Bar

| Company | Differentiator |
|---------|----------------|
| Stripe | 4 yrs integrating their API in production |
| Ramp | Public engineering blog on their React perf work |
| Linear | Power user, can speak to specific UX decisions |
| Vercel | Next.js is core stack; deployed 12+ projects |
| Figma | Plugin development experience matches their platform push |
| Notion | Built internal tools mimicking their block model |

### Effort Reallocation

The 12 demoted jobs free up ~6 hours of deep customization time. Options:
1. **Go deeper on Tier 1** — add portfolio case studies tailored to each
2. **Expand Tier 1 search** — scan Wellfound and YC job board for missed matches
3. **Prep follow-up sequences** — draft week-2 and week-4 nudges now

Which direction?
judge_reasoning: The chatbot directly followed the user's instruction to tighten the criteria, provided a detailed breakdown of the new scoring logic, and updated the job counts accordingly.
